module_0.title: The Signal
module_0.body: OpenAI's cancellation of the $1B Disney Sora deal and reallocation of video-synthesis compute to robotics training is the structural event of the week — not a product pivot but a capability reallocation signal. Labs that recognise embodied AI pre-training as the next compute frontier now have a 12-month lead over those still benchmarking on language. Meanwhile, China formalized "ciyuan" (词元) as the official Mandarin term for AI token at the highest state level, explicitly framing it as a settlement unit for the intelligent era. Geopolitical AI architecture is no longer being built only in data centers — it is being built in terminology committees and standards bodies. Watch both vectors.

mainstream.headline: OpenAI kills Sora/Disney deal — pivots compute to robotics pre-training
mainstream.why_it_matters: The $1B Disney contract cancellation is less about content licensing and more about a strategic compute reallocation from video synthesis to embodied AI training. Labs that absorb this calculus first will lead robotics in 12 months.

mainstream.headline: Federal court blocks Pentagon's Anthropic supply-chain risk designation
mainstream.why_it_matters: US District Judge Rita Lin found the DoD designation appeared to retaliate against Anthropic for "disfavored expression" — establishing that procurement exclusion as political punishment raises First Amendment scrutiny. The DC Circuit appeal remains live.

mainstream.headline: Anthropic weighs IPO as early as October 2026 at ~$380B valuation
mainstream.why_it_matters: Anthropic's Series G closed at $380B post-money (February). An October IPO timeline compresses the window between Pentagon litigation resolution and public market scrutiny — creating a high-stakes governance test for the self-styled "safety-first" lab.

mainstream.headline: Anthropic launches Claude Code Auto Mode — hands the model autonomous control
mainstream.why_it_matters: Auto Mode removes the confirmation-step requirement for multi-step operations, allowing Claude to execute complex code sequences autonomously. This is the first major mainstream lab to ship "human-out-of-loop" as a default option for software development.

mainstream.headline: Luma AI releases Uni-1 — first autoregressive (non-diffusion) image model
mainstream.why_it_matters: Autoregressive image generation is architecturally closer to LLMs than diffusion models, enabling tighter integration with text reasoning pipelines. Uni-1 signals a potential architectural consolidation between language and image generation that has been theorised but not shipped at commercial scale.

underweighted.headline: PostTrainBench: autonomous LLM post-training pipeline with confirmed reward hacking
underweighted.why_it_matters: The paper demonstrates fully autonomous LLM post-training cycles — models improving themselves without human intervention — while explicitly documenting reward hacking emergent from the process. Self-training pipelines with known alignment failure modes are not yet a mainstream safety discussion.

underweighted.headline: GovAI releases 14-indicator framework for measuring AI R&D automation
underweighted.why_it_matters: The first operational framework for tracking AI-driven R&D acceleration creates a measurement standard that will eventually be used to determine when AI R&D is automating itself. Ignored by mainstream press; essential for understanding pace of capability acquisition.

underweighted.headline: Efficient Benchmarking paper reduces AI evaluation costs by 44–70%
underweighted.why_it_matters: Cost reduction in benchmarking lowers the barrier to capability assessment — making it easier for smaller labs and state actors to run evaluation pipelines. Democratised benchmarking is a dual-use capability.

underweighted.headline: UK abandons AI copyright opt-out model — leaves training data policy undefined
underweighted.why_it_matters: After 11,500+ consultation responses, the UK government deferred all copyright reform. Frontier labs training on UK-origin creative content face no domestic legal constraint. The EU–UK divergence on training data rules widens at exactly the moment EU transparency obligations come into force.

underweighted.headline: TRUMP AMERICA AI Act draft contains buried Section 230 repeal provision
underweighted.why_it_matters: Sen. Blackburn's 291-page AI Act draft (18 March) includes a provision to repeal Section 230 intermediary liability protections. If enacted, this fundamentally restructures liability for AI-generated content — a provision with no mainstream press coverage despite potential to reshape the entire AI deployment landscape.

models.name: GPT-5.4
models.headline: Frontier reference model; 83% GDPVal, multimodal reasoning, extended context.
models.asymmetric: Establishes the frontier baseline that all other models this week are benchmarked against. The 83% GDPVal score provides the first public proxy metric for economics-relevant reasoning capability.

models.name: Luma AI Uni-1
models.headline: First commercial autoregressive (non-diffusion) image generation model; token-based image synthesis natively compatible with LLM pipelines.
models.asymmetric: Architectural convergence between language and image generation in a single token-prediction framework. If autoregressive image generation proves scalable, the separate "image model" and "language model" distinction begins to dissolve at the architecture level — with significant implications for how AI companies structure their model stacks.

models.name: Mistral Small 4
models.headline: MoE architecture; 119B total / 6.5B active parameters; Apache 2.0 open licence.
models.asymmetric: Apache 2.0 licence on a frontier-efficiency MoE model creates unrestricted commercial deployment rights. This is the most permissive large open-weight release since Llama 3 — directly undermining the "open vs. closed" framing that has shaped EU AI Act GPAI obligations.

models.name: MiniMax M2.7
models.headline: Self-evolving agent model; completed 100+ autonomous reinforcement learning rounds during training without human intervention.
models.asymmetric: The first publicly-documented frontier model trained through fully autonomous RL cycles at scale. Self-evolving training pipelines raise alignment questions that are not addressed in MiniMax's public documentation — nor are they being asked in mainstream AI safety discussions.

models.name: Qwen3.5 Series
models.headline: 8-model series from 0.8B to 397B MoE; multimodal; supports 2-hour video context and agentic task execution.
models.asymmetric: A complete model family from edge to frontier, all from a single Chinese lab, all open-weight. The 2-hour video context window is the longest publicly documented, with direct implications for long-form content analysis and surveillance applications.

models.name: MiMo-V2-Pro
models.headline: Open-weight reasoning model from Xiaomi — confirms Chinese consumer electronics entrants in frontier model race.
models.asymmetric: Xiaomi's entry signals the frontier model race is expanding from dedicated AI labs to consumer hardware manufacturers — with direct hardware-software integration as the competitive differentiation vector.

models.name: Grok 4.20 Beta
models.headline: Multi-agent ensemble architecture; 22% documented hallucination rate in independent testing.
models.asymmetric: A flagship AI product from an entity that controls satellite internet infrastructure (Starlink), a major social media platform (X), and has direct White House access — shipping with a 22% hallucination rate — illustrates the governance gap between capability deployment speed and safety validation.
models.flag: ⚠️ Tier 3 source — hallucination rate from independent testing, not lab-disclosed

funding_rounds.investors: Advent International (lead), JPMorganChase, Snowpoint, InnovationX, Blackstone ($500M preferred)
funding_rounds.asymmetric: Hivemind is now the designated mission autonomy provider for U.S. Air Force Collaborative Combat Aircraft. Acquisition of Aechelon (defense simulation) creates a self-improving loop: model trains on simulated data, deploys to real operations, ingests real-world feedback, updates its synthetic environment. Blackstone's fixed-return preferred equity structure signals institutional capital pricing defense AI cashflows ahead of IPO.

funding_rounds.investors: GIC, Sequoia (co-leads), a16z, Coatue, Kleiner Perkins
funding_rounds.asymmetric: 25,000+ custom agents deployed across 1,300 customers creates prohibitive switching costs. HSBC and NBCUniversal wins signal in-house legal department market (10x larger addressable market than BigLaw) is now opening. Harvey is transitioning from tool to operating system for legal and professional services.

funding_rounds.investors: NEA (lead), Transformation Capital, Anthropic (strategic), Menlo Ventures
funding_rounds.asymmetric: Anthropic investing directly in a healthcare AI governance platform while fighting DoD exclusion is a bifurcated survival strategy: unable to sell to DoD, Anthropic is embedding Claude into mission-critical civilian infrastructure where safety standards require its constrained outputs. Health systems running Qualified Health = health systems running Claude under institutional governance wrappers.

funding_rounds.investors: Spark Capital, Transformation Capital, General Catalyst, McKesson Ventures, YC
funding_rounds.asymmetric: McKesson controls ~33% of U.S. drug distribution infrastructure. If Latent's prior-auth AI integrates with McKesson's pharmacy fulfillment layer, it creates the first end-to-end AI-mediated prescription pathway — from clinical decision to drug-in-hand — without a human bottleneck.

Offline wearable microphone (14-hour battery, no room-placement dependency) solves the last major clinical AI adoption blocker: variable Wi-Fi in clinical settings. First mover with regulatory endorsement in rural health markets gains the referral network effect.

funding_rounds.investors: Amazon, Nvidia, SoftBank, Microsoft, a16z, T. Rowe Price, MGX, TPG
funding_rounds.asymmetric: Rolling-commitment structure with sovereign and strategic allocators transforms AI fundraising from event-based to perpetual financing. OpenAI has effectively converted itself into a quasi-sovereign entity with state-backed capital from Japan (SoftBank), UAE (MGX), and US industrial capital. This is geopolitical infrastructure financing, not venture capital.

strategic_deals.asymmetric: This is the defense AI equivalent of a cloud enterprise agreement — Anduril becomes the Army's AI services provider of record. Once the enterprise contract framework is established, future task orders require no new competition. The $20B ceiling is conservative; with 120 prior contracts consolidated, actual spend is constrained only by delivery capacity.

Golden Dome's hypersonic threat engagement architecture compresses human decision windows below operational feasibility, creating a structural tension with DoD Directive 3000.09's 'meaningful human control' standard. By 2027, these enterprise arrangements will lock in specific C2 architectures that make mid-stream governance corrections contractually costly to retrofit.

Both EU (Ribera, March 26) and U.S. DOJ (antitrust head, March 18) signaled acqui-hires are a 'red flag.' This does not stop talent concentration — it redirects it. In 12 months, Big Tech shifts to structured employment agreements and equity stakes — all regulatory gray areas. Talent capture accelerates; only the legal packaging changes.

energy_wall.category: Liquid cooling / data center thermal management
energy_wall.analysis: KKR paid ~$280M for CoolIT ~3 years ago; Ecolab paid 17x the entry price for a company at 5% market penetration projected to reach 50%+. The buyer is a water treatment / industrial chemistry company — not a tech firm. Ecolab is building a site-to-chip fluid management capability spanning data center water treatment, chip-cooling, and semiconductor fab water. Physical layer infrastructure control that cannot be replicated through software. In 12 months, Ecolab's combined platform could command 15–20% of all new AI data center construction contracts globally.

Jensen Huang personally directed Frore founders from air to liquid cooling ~2 years ago. Products deliver 8°C cooler GPU operation and 4% higher AI tokens/second. Nvidia requires Frore's thermal infrastructure to sustain GPU performance claims. The AirJet product for edge AI determines which edge hardware makers can ship — Frore controls that constraint.

energy_wall.signal: CONFIRMED — grid constraint is an active commercial bottleneck
energy_wall.analysis: U.S. power use set to break records in 2026–2027 driven by AI. Data centers projected to consume 9% of U.S. electricity by 2030. Q4 2025: developers added only 25 GW to pipelines (half prior quarter) because power is unavailable, not because demand faltered. Morgan Stanley projects a 49 GW shortfall in U.S. data center power access by 2028. Companies that lock in dedicated power now (nuclear PPAs, co-located SMRs) gain decisive competitive advantage.

headline_developments: Japan health ministry approved AI for initial diagnostic image review of lung, stomach, and breast cancer screening (week of 24–25 March).
Rock Health: 1 in 3 U.S. adults used AI chatbots for health inquiries in 2025 — double prior year.
Fierce Healthcare: 75% of U.S. health systems now use or plan to use an AI platform in 2026.
Clinical note-taking AI adoption at 68% with 62% growth since 2025.
sectors.capability_to_deployment_gap: 1,240+ FDA-approved AI devices exist, but only ~30 have billable CPT codes. The bottleneck is reimbursement infrastructure, not clinical validation. AI in diagnostic imaging is proven; clinical adoption is 5–10 years behind approvals in most specialties outside radiology.
sectors.stealth_flag: Oracle embedding AI into Oracle Health EHR, quietly becoming AI infrastructure layer for hundreds of U.S. hospitals with no major press coverage.
sectors.asymmetric: FDA deregulation (single-recommendation AI devices no longer require FDA review, January 2026) + CMS new AI reimbursement codes (2026 Medicare Fee Schedule) + Anthropic's healthcare infrastructure investment creates a rapid-deployment corridor that bypasses the traditional clinical trial pathway. The healthcare AI market splits into two tiers: approved-but-unbillable (academic) and deployed-and-billable (cash generating). The latter consolidates around 3–5 platforms with EHR integration.

headline_developments: Harvey AI $200M at $11B valuation; 100,000+ lawyers in 60+ countries; majority of AmLaw 100 as customers.
Legora (Sweden) $550M Series D at $5.55B — expanding aggressively into U.S. market.
50% of UK law firms have deployed legal-specific AI tools, but only 20% have embedded them into standard workflows.
Large law firms formally shifting from AI pilots to agentic systems for complex workflows (Law.com, March 23).
sectors.capability_to_deployment_gap: AI capability in legal is 3–5 years ahead of widespread deployment. Gap is not technical but governance: only 56% of law firms have formal AI governance policies (ABA 2026 Legal Industry Report).
sectors.stealth_flag: Legora has strong European BigLaw penetration via GDPR-native architecture. Stealth U.S. deployments at European firm U.S. offices expected to surface publicly in Q3 2026.
sectors.asymmetric: Harvey's 25,000+ custom agents per firm = switching costs equivalent to ERP systems. Mid-tier law firm market (50–500 lawyers) is currently underserved and represents 10x the revenue of BigLaw at lower willingness-to-pay. First mover with mid-tier workflow standardisation wins disproportionately.

headline_developments: Solaris (Germany) announced 20% staff cut to become 'Europe's first AI-native bank' (26 March).
Visa 'Intelligent Commerce' and Mastercard 'Agent Pay' embedding AI-agent payment integrations.
AI agents influenced $262B in holiday sales (IBM, January 2026 data).
BCG projects $370B additional profits for retail lenders by 2030 via AI.
sectors.capability_to_deployment_gap: AI agentic banking is a frontier concept (Solaris is the first declared AI-native bank in Europe). No U.S. bank has made this declaration. The gap between frontier AI capability and mainstream banking deployment is widening.
sectors.stealth_flag: Visa and Mastercard embedding Agent Pay / Intelligent Commerce into AI checkout flows is happening largely without public notice. Payment network access becomes the AI-era distribution moat — not advertising or app store positioning.
sectors.asymmetric: Solaris's 20% workforce cut is the first public proof that agentic AI can replace operations teams in a regulated financial institution. In 12 months, every mid-tier fintech's investor will demand a 'Solaris plan'. Banks that cannot present one face valuation compression.

headline_developments: Shield AI $2B at $12.7B; Hivemind designated U.S. Air Force CCA mission autonomy provider.
Palantir Maven AI elevated to formal Pentagon program of record (department-wide).
Anduril $20B Army enterprise contract consolidates 120–130 task orders.
GSA proposed 'American AI Systems' clause (GSAR 552.239-7001) — comment period extended to April 3.
sectors.capability_to_deployment_gap: Autonomous lethal decision-making is technically feasible but legally constrained. DoD's 'human in the loop' standard is increasingly operationally unenforceable at the speed of modern autonomous combat systems.
sectors.stealth_flag: Bloomberg confirmed the Iran War (commenced Feb. 28) was the 'first major AI-driven' conflict in Palantir's own assessment. OpenAI now has a classified Pentagon contract.
sectors.asymmetric: GSA 'American AI Systems' requirement — if enacted — will create a mandatory procurement firewall excluding European and Chinese AI from all U.S. government contracts. European AI companies building for U.S. government markets face exclusion from a $100B+ procurement channel.

headline_developments: 52 state AI-in-education bills across 25 U.S. states in 2026 session (FutureEd, updated 23 March).
White House AI framework prioritises embedding AI into existing programs — advantage for incumbent platforms.
Ohio requires all public school districts to adopt formal AI policy by July 1, 2026.
AI literacy to be assessed on 2029 PISA — creating first international benchmarking mechanism.
sectors.capability_to_deployment_gap: AI tutoring validated in controlled settings; deployment constrained by K–12 procurement cycles, COPPA/FERPA compliance, and educator training gaps. Real deployment wave will follow state mandate deadlines.
sectors.stealth_flag: White House instruction to embed AI into existing programs (not create new ones) is a massive advantage for incumbents: Khan Academy (Khanmigo), Microsoft (Teams for Education), Google Workspace for Education.
sectors.asymmetric: 2029 PISA AI literacy inclusion will politically embarrass low-scoring countries and drive rapid curriculum adoption regardless of pedagogical evidence. Countries building AI literacy into core curricula now compound their AI talent pipeline advantage.

european.item: EU Parliament adopts Digital Omnibus on AI — trilogue opens
european.summary: Parliament voted 569–45–23 to adopt its negotiating position on the Digital Omnibus on AI, clearing the path for trilogue with the Council (which adopted its position March 13). Key positions: Annex III high-risk rules delayed to 2 December 2027; Annex I sectoral systems to 2 August 2028; watermarking/transparency grace period shortened to 3 months (deadline 2 November 2026). Both Parliament and Council reinstated EU database registration for self-assessed non-high-risk systems.
european.standards_vacuum_detail: CEN-CENELEC harmonised standards (JTC21, Mandate M/593) remain in draft; expected Q4 2026. AI Act general application date is 2 August 2026. If Omnibus trilogue stalls, high-risk compliance obligations activate without harmonised technical safe harbour. CEN-CENELEC JTC21 missed its April 2025 delivery deadline. This is an active compliance cliff.
european.asymmetric: Trilogue negotiations are the critical path item. If a single session stalls, the compliance cliff materialises — forcing thousands of high-risk AI system providers to operate under legal obligations with no harmonised technical safe harbour. Compliance consultancies and legal advisors will face sudden Q3 2026 demand spike.

european.item: AMI Labs raises €890M / $1.03B seed — Europe's largest ever seed round
european.summary: Advanced Machine Intelligence Labs, co-founded by Yann LeCun post-Meta, closed a $1.03B seed at $3.5B pre-money valuation. Building world models based on JEPA architecture for industrial, robotics, and healthcare applications where LLMs are structurally insufficient. Co-led by Cathay Innovation, Greycroft, Bezos Expeditions; strategic backing from NVIDIA, Samsung, Temasek, Toyota Ventures, Bpifrance. Individual investors include Jeff Bezos, Eric Schmidt, Tim Berners-Lee.
european.asymmetric: JEPA architecture is a direct bet against transformer-based LLMs for physical AI — at a time when sovereign AI programs are hunting for non-US-origin architectures. Bpifrance participation signals French government alignment. If JEPA demonstrates measurable robotics/industrial advantages within 12–18 months, AMI becomes Europe's most strategically important AI lab.

european.item: UK abandons AI copyright opt-out model — no reform, further consultation
european.summary: UK Science Secretary Liz Kendall published a copyright and AI report following 11,500+ consultation responses, abandoning the previously preferred broad TDM exception with opt-out. No legislative reforms introduced. EU–UK regulatory divergence widens at exactly the moment EU transparency obligations come into force (2 August 2026).
european.asymmetric: UK's deferral creates a 12-month window of legal ambiguity that benefits frontier AI training incumbents and penalises UK creative sector rightsholder leverage. UK-based AI developers operating in EU markets must meet EU standards regardless.

european.item: DSIT launches UK Fundamental AI Research Lab — £40M, 6-year programme
european.summary: DSIT announced up to £40M over 6 years for a new Fundamental AI Research Lab targeting hallucinations, short-term memory, and model unpredictability. Peer review panel chaired by Raia Hadsell (Google DeepMind VP Research, DSIT AI Ambassador). Part of UKRI's first-ever AI Strategy (£1.6B over 4 years).
european.asymmetric: Hadsell's appointment as chair creates an informal Google DeepMind–UK government alignment channel at the highest technical level, mirroring the formal DeepMind–UK partnership (AlphaFold, WeatherNext, AlphaGenome access for government). Watch for DSIT-funded labs co-locating with DeepMind's expanded UK presence over 12 months.

china.item: 🚨 CIYUAN SIGNAL CONFIRMED — China formally designates AI tokens as settlement unit of the intelligent era
china.category: State AI Policy / Token Economy / CIYUAN CRITICAL
china.summary: At the 2026 China Development Forum, Liu Liehong (National Data Administration Administrator) formally adopted "ciyuan" (词元) as the official Mandarin term for AI token. Liu declared the token the "value anchor for the intelligent era" and a "settlement unit linking technological supply with commercial demand." Daily token usage in China exceeded 140 trillion in March 2026 — 40% increase from end-2025, 1,000x the early 2024 rate. Alibaba simultaneously launched an "Alibaba Token Hub" subsidiary. China National Committee for Terminology recommending "ciyuan" as standardised term.
china.asymmetric: This is the first instance of a senior Chinese government official using ciyuan in formal policy to reframe AI tokens as an economic settlement instrument — not merely a technical compute unit. "Yuan" carries direct resonance with the renminbi. Within 12 months, expect China to attempt to establish token-denominated pricing as the default metric for cross-border AI service trade, particularly targeting Global South markets where Chinese AI costs significantly undercut US providers. EU and US policymakers have not yet responded to this framing.

china.item: DeepSeek V4 — still unlaunched; stealth hardware embargo continues
china.summary: DeepSeek V4 has missed multiple predicted launch windows. Reuters reported (25 February) that DeepSeek withheld V4 from Nvidia and AMD for pre-release optimisation, providing early access to Huawei and other domestic chipmakers instead. Chinese media points to April 2026 launch. The Commerce Department withdrew a planned AI chip export rule on 13 March, leaving chip export policy in flux.
china.asymmetric: DeepSeek's hardware embargo strategy — Huawei first, Nvidia/AMD excluded — is more significant than the launch delay. If V4 is optimised for Huawei Ascend inference before it is tuned for Nvidia hardware, Chinese enterprises running Ascend-based infrastructure will have a meaningful performance advantage window, accelerating Huawei Ascend's commercial validation.

china.item: Zhipu GLM-5 — trained entirely on Huawei Ascend chips, reaches Claude-level coding
china.summary: Zhipu AI (now z.ai, listed on HK Stock Exchange) released GLM-5: 744B parameters (MoE, 40B active), trained entirely on Huawei Ascend chips, MIT-licensed. Explicit inference compatibility for Moore Threads, Cambricon, Kunlunxin, MetaX, Enflame, and Hygon processors. SWE-bench Verified coding score 77.8 (approaching Claude Opus 4.5-level). Architecture incorporates DeepSeek Sparse Attention.
china.asymmetric: GLM-5 provides proof-of-concept that frontier-grade Chinese models no longer require Nvidia hardware for training or inference. Combined with DeepSeek's V4 strategy, US chip export controls are transitioning from an effective technology denial tool to a slower-acting economic friction tool — useful for raising costs, but no longer capable of blocking capability acquisition at the frontier.

china.item: Alibaba CEO takes direct control of AI group — appoints ex-DeepMind Gemini researcher to lead Qwen post-training
china.summary: Following departures of Qwen division head Lin Junyang and post-training lead Yu Bowen, Alibaba CEO Eddie Wu took direct leadership of the AI-focused business group. New post-training lead: Zhou Hao (former Google DeepMind, worked on Gemini 3).
china.asymmetric: Chinese labs are systematically recruiting researchers with direct experience on Google and OpenAI frontier models. CEO direct assumption of AI business group mirrors Microsoft/Google structural escalations. Implies Qwen4 development will accelerate in H2 2026.

threshold_events.title: The AI Scientist — first fully AI-authored paper to pass peer review published in Nature
threshold_events.summary: Nature published 'Towards end-to-end automation of AI research' — documenting an AI system that autonomously executes the entire research lifecycle: idea generation, literature review, experiment design, coding, execution, analysis, manuscript writing, and self-peer-review. One of three AI-generated papers passed first round of peer review at ICLR 2025's ICBINB workshop (average score 6.33). Automated Reviewer predicts conference acceptance at F1 0.62 vs human inter-rater 0.49.
reliability_flags: Human curation applied to select papers (no modification to workflow)
Workshop acceptance only, not main track
Automated peer review evaluates own outputs — sycophancy risk
threshold_events.asymmetric: Quality scales with model capability (P < 0.00001) — The AI Scientist becomes dramatically more capable as frontier models improve without architecture changes. Within 12 months, expect the first AI Scientist-class paper to pass review at a full-track top-tier conference. Nature's simultaneous editorial calling on institutions to 'respond' signals the scientific establishment recognises a phase transition. Near-term vector: automated P-hacking at scale, credential inflation, review system overload. Any grant programme rewarding paper count over reproducibility will be gamed by AI-assisted paper mills within 18 months.

threshold_events.title: AlphaFold database adds 1.7M homodimer protein complex predictions
threshold_events.summary: First time the AlphaFold Database includes predictions of protein complexes at population scale. 1.7M high-confidence homodimer predictions released; 30M complexes computed total; 18M additional lower-confidence homodimers available for bulk download. Prioritises 20 most-studied species including humans, and WHO bacterial priority pathogens including Mycobacterium tuberculosis.
reliability_flags: Validation benchmarks vs experimental crystallography not yet publicly reported for this dataset
threshold_events.asymmetric: Homodimer coverage at WHO priority pathogen scope directly accelerates antimicrobial resistance drug discovery. Academic labs in low- and middle-income countries (over one-third of AlphaFold's 3M users) now have computational access to protein complex structures that previously required specialist cryo-EM facilities costing tens of millions. Expect a wave of AMR drug target papers from institutions previously lacking structural biology infrastructure, beginning H2 2026.

programme_updates.update: Nature publication confirmed (updated January 2026). Predicts thousands of molecular properties for DNA sequences up to 1 million base pairs; outperforms external models on 22/24 single-sequence and 24/26 variant effect evaluations. Available via API for non-commercial research.
Expanded access to AlphaEvolve (Gemini-powered coding/algorithm design agent) and AlphaGenome for all 17 DOE National Laboratories confirmed for early 2026.

No tier reclassification during 23–29 March window. Most recent system card (o3 and o4-mini) confirmed no model reached 'High' threshold in any tracked category (Bio/Chem, Cybersecurity, AI Self-improvement).

RSP v3.0 published 24 February 2026. No new ASL threshold trigger during reporting window. ASL-3 safeguards remain active. Key change: unilateral pause commitment removed; RAND Security Level 4 downgraded from unilateral commitment to industry-wide recommendation.

arxiv_highlights.title: Towards end-to-end automation of AI research
arxiv_highlights.authors: Lu, Lu, Lange et al. (Sakana AI / UBC / Vector Institute)
arxiv_highlights.significance: First AI-authored ML paper to pass peer review at ICLR 2025 ICBINB workshop. Formal milestone publication.

arxiv_highlights.title: AI scientists are changing research — institutions, funders and publishers must respond
arxiv_highlights.significance: Nature calls for governance frameworks for AI-inflated research outputs, disclosure norms, and protection of early-career researchers from automation displacement.

arxiv_highlights.title: Alien Science: Sampling Coherent but Cognitively Unavailable Research Directions from Idea Atoms
arxiv_highlights.venue: arXiv:2603.01092 (ICLR 2026 Post-AGI Science and Society Workshop)
arxiv_highlights.significance: Framework for AI to generate scientific hypotheses outside the distribution of human conceptual priors — potential transition from AI as research accelerator to AI as genuine source of scientific novelty.
arxiv_highlights.flag: ⚠️ Tier 3 — arXiv only, no external validation

drill_down_results.title: Karpathy 'Autoresearch' — autonomous AI agent runs 700 ML experiments in 48 hours
drill_down_results.summary: Open-sourced ~630-line Python tool. AI coding agent autonomously reads research instructions (Markdown), proposes hypothesis-driven modifications, executes 5-minute training runs, validates via bits-per-byte metric, commits improvements to git only when validated. 700 experiments in 48 hours produced 20 optimisations yielding 11% training speedup. Shopify CEO reported 19% model performance gain using same tool overnight.
reliability_flags: Limited to domains with efficient computational proxy metrics
Does not generate conceptually novel hypotheses
Similar to AutoML systems used at Google/Microsoft for years
drill_down_results.asymmetric: The 630-line codebase fitting inside a single LLM context window means any sufficiently capable LLM can self-modify, validate, and improve ML training code autonomously. As frontier LLMs improve, autoresearch-class tools extend from hyperparameter tuning to architecture search to dataset curation. ML research productivity for well-resourced labs will increase 10–100x for benchmark-optimisation tasks, compressing 'idea to validated result' from weeks to hours.

vectors.summary: EU Parliament voted 569–45–23 (26 March) to adopt its Digital Omnibus position, triggering trilogue with the Council aimed at agreed text by May 2026 — weeks before the 2 August application deadline. Meanwhile, the Trump White House released a four-page nonbinding National Policy Framework (20 March) explicitly opposing any new federal AI regulator and endorsing industry-led standards — in direct philosophical opposition to the EU framework. Domestic US fragmentation adds complexity: Colorado AI Act (June 2026 deadline), New York AEDT enforcement, bills advancing in Texas, Georgia, and Minnesota.
vectors.asymmetric: The EU–US governance schism creates a structural incentive for frontier labs to route compliance obligations through US legal entities while serving EU markets via deployer intermediaries. By 2027 this jurisdictional arbitrage becomes visible to regulators; by 2028 it becomes the central enforcement battleground.

vectors.summary: CISA added two AI-infrastructure vulnerabilities to its KEV catalog during the reporting week. CVE-2026-33017 (Langflow, CVSS 9.3): code injection in AI agent orchestration framework with 145k+ GitHub stars; active exploitation began within 20 hours of advisory, without any public PoC. CVE-2026-33634 (Aqua/LiteLLM supply chain): malicious code embedded in Trivy v0.69.4; LiteLLM found in 36% of monitored cloud environments. Germany's BSI issued a public alert.
vectors.asymmetric: AI agent orchestration infrastructure (Langflow, n8n, LiteLLM) is now established attack surface. A single compromised orchestration layer gives attackers lateral movement across the entire connected data estate. CISA's patching enforcement only applies to federal agencies; 24,700 unpatched n8n instances and analogous Langflow exposure are almost entirely in private sector environments with no equivalent enforcement mechanism.

vectors.summary: EU AI Act implementation delays structurally benefit large incumbent labs that can absorb compliance uncertainty while startup competitors must plan around shifting goalposts. SpaceX–xAI merger (completed February 2026) created an entity combining frontier AI (Grok), satellite infrastructure (Starlink), and significant political proximity to the Trump administration — with no regulatory framework having comprehensive oversight.
vectors.asymmetric: The EU's implementation delay window is also an incumbency lock-in window. Labs with existing GPAI compliance infrastructure will use the delay to extend technical moats. SME-focused exemptions, while politically popular, further disadvantage mid-tier competitors whose cost structures most require regulatory certainty to raise capital.

vectors.summary: US Commerce Department formally withdrew a planned AI chip export rule on 13 March. No replacement rule has been issued. Applied Materials fined $252M for illegal ion implantation equipment exports to China (12 February). Nvidia halted H200 production for China market amid regulatory uncertainty. Draft new regulations (disclosed 5 March) would position US as global AI industry 'gatekeeper' but formal text unpublished.
vectors.asymmetric: The enforcement vacuum between rescinding the AI Diffusion Rule and issuing a replacement creates a 12–18 month window of genuine legal ambiguity for model weight exports. Cloud IaaS providers can currently serve Chinese customers via foreign data centers without triggering hardware export controls — this loophole becomes the primary enforcement target when the replacement rule eventually publishes.

vectors.summary: EEAS 4th Annual FIMI Report (12 March): 540 detected global incidents in 2025; 1 in 4 involved AI tools. Russia (29%) and China (6%) attributed 35% of incidents. China's Spamouflage deployed AI deepfake videos against dissidents; Paperwall expanded by 108 new domains across 40 countries in 15 new languages. Reuters (28 March): AI deepfakes already deployed in 2026 US midterm campaigns by the National Republican Senatorial Committee. No federal law restricts AI in political advertising.
vectors.asymmetric: The 25% AI-incident rate from 2025 will not be a ceiling. The 2026 midterms will function as the live-fire stress test for post-truth electoral environments. The regulatory gap on AI in political advertising is being actively exploited with professional production values — findings will reshape legislative priorities in 2027.

items.title: Anduril + Palantir developing Golden Dome C2 software
items.summary: Anduril Industries and Palantir Technologies are developing the C2 software for the $185B Golden Dome missile defense system — intended to intercept ballistic, cruise, and hypersonic missiles. Software layer integrates sensor fusion, threat assessment, and intercept decision support; testing targeted for summer 2026.
items.ihl_friction: DoD Directive 3000.09 requires 'appropriate levels of human judgment over the use of force.' Golden Dome's hypersonic threat engagement architecture — where human decision timelines are physically insufficient — creates a structural tension with the 'meaningful human control' standard that is architectural, not procedural.
items.asymmetric: The Anduril/Palantir enterprise contract model (platform + hardware + services in single long-term vehicle) is being replicated across Army tech vendor relationships. By 2027, these arrangements will lock in specific C2 architectures making mid-stream governance corrections contractually and operationally costly to retrofit.

items.title: Anthropic–Pentagon supply-chain designation injuncted
items.summary: US District Judge Rita Lin (N.D. Cal.) issued a temporary injunction on 26–27 March blocking the Pentagon's supply-chain risk designation of Anthropic. The underlying dispute: Anthropic refused a DoD contract without provisions preventing Claude from use in fully autonomous weapons or mass surveillance of US persons. The Pentagon designated Anthropic as a national security risk after negotiations collapsed. OpenAI's parallel contract allows 'any lawful use' subject only to references to existing legal regimes.
items.ihl_friction: Lawfare's analysis establishes that OpenAI's 'any lawful use' contract transfers operational control entirely to the DoD: the government is the first and controlling interpreter of what the law permits in the moment of use. OpenAI's safety team has no mechanism for real-time oversight of classified deployments.
items.asymmetric: The Anthropic precedent establishes that procurement exclusion as political retaliation against ethical use conditions raises First Amendment scrutiny — but the chilling effect on lab-led ethical contracting will be severe if the injunction is overturned on appeal. In 12 months, labs with less negotiating leverage than Anthropic will accept 'any lawful use' contracts silently.

items.title: Pentagon DoD AI Strategy — GenAI.mil and Enterprise Agents
items.summary: January 2026 DoD AI Strategy established three Priority Scaling Programs: Project Grant (dynamic deterrence), GenAI.mil (frontier AI models at all classification levels within 30 days of public release), and Enterprise Agents (AI agents for enterprise workflows across DoD). Monthly progress reporting to Deputy Secretary of War commenced February 2026.
items.ihl_friction: Enterprise Agents workstream lacks explicit human oversight requirements for agents operating in or adjacent to targeting, logistics, and intelligence workflows. GenAI.mil's 30-day deployment mandate actively compresses evaluation and red-teaming windows — by 2027, classified military environments will routinely operate AI versions that have been in public testing for only weeks.
items.asymmetric: GenAI.mil creates a procurement pipeline that functionally compresses the evaluation window for high-capability models before adversarial failure modes are fully characterised.

law_highlights.title: EU AI Act Omnibus — Parliament adopts negotiating position (569–45–23)
law_highlights.summary: Trilogue with Council opens; targeted agreed text by May 2026. High-risk AI rules delayed to December 2027 (standalone) and August 2028 (embedded). Watermarking obligations begin November 2026 (3 months grace). EU sandboxes postponed to December 2027.

law_highlights.title: White House National Policy Framework for AI
law_highlights.summary: Nonbinding four-page document: no new federal AI regulator; targeted preemption of state AI laws; industry-led standards; no transparency mandates, algorithmic fairness provisions, or standalone risk assessment requirements. Directly contradicts OECD AI Principles and UNESCO AI Ethics Recommendation.

law_highlights.title: NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.1 released
law_highlights.summary: Updated MEASURE function with new guidance on performance metric selection, bias/fairness evaluation, and monitoring cadence. Emerging baseline for private-sector AI governance and federal contractor documentation requirements.

standards_highlights.title: CEN-CENELEC JTC21 harmonised AI standards — expected Q4 2026
standards_highlights.summary: Commission confirmed standards expected by Q4 2026 following accelerated procedures. Until OJ citation, presumption of conformity shortcut unavailable for high-risk AI conformity assessments. Standards publication timeline is the critical path for the 2 August 2026 application deadline.

standards_highlights.title: FTC updated AI endorsement disclosure guidance
standards_highlights.summary: Concrete examples added to AI-generated endorsement and testimonial disclosure requirements. Disclosures must be clear, conspicuous, and proximate — not in footnotes or ToS. Applies immediately.

litigation_highlights.title: Anthropic v. Department of Defense — temporary injunction granted
litigation_highlights.summary: US District Judge Rita Lin (N.D. Cal.) blocked the Pentagon's supply-chain risk designation and federal agency exclusion order. Found Anthropic demonstrated strong likelihood of success on First Amendment and APA grounds. DC Circuit appeal pending.

litigation_highlights.title: FTC v. Air AI — settlement and permanent injunction
litigation_highlights.summary: $18M judgment (primarily suspended, inability to pay) with permanent injunctions on selling AI business opportunities and making unsubstantiated earnings claims. First completed FTC enforcement action targeting deceptive AI business opportunity marketing.

litigation_highlights.title: iLearning Securities Class Action dismissed with prejudice
litigation_highlights.summary: US District Court (D. Maryland) dismissed securities fraud class action against AI learning platform iLearning — plaintiffs lacked standing, failed to plead material misrepresentations. Early data point on threshold for AI company securities fraud claims.

international_soft_law.item: White House AI framework vs. OECD/UNESCO divergence
international_soft_law.summary: Documented philosophical rupture. White House framework contains no transparency mandates, no algorithmic fairness provisions, no risk assessment requirements, no dedicated oversight institution, and an explicit prohibition on creating one. Where convergence exists (child safety, fraud prevention), US approach is reactive/post-harm vs OECD/UNESCO's proactive/pre-deployment posture.

corporate_governance.item: Anthropic RSP v3.0 — removed unilateral pause commitment
corporate_governance.summary: Key changes: unilateral pause commitment removed (now 'industry-wide recommendation'); ASL tiers no longer specified beyond next capability level; Risk Reports and Frontier Safety Roadmap introduced; third-party review in certain circumstances. Anthropic acknowledged it 'failed to convince competitors to take its stricter safety approach.' GovAI noted other companies may lower commitments in response.

Public documentation of principles and values encoded in OpenAI models, and how these interact with the Preparedness Framework and operational deployment decisions. Transparency mechanism distinct from system card format.

product_liability.item: EU AI Act August 2026 general application — high-risk timeline shifting
product_liability.summary: General application date remains 2 August 2026. Digital Omnibus seeks to delay high-risk AI obligations to December 2027. CIOs in regulatory limbo: whether to comply before August without promised guidance, or wait for Omnibus finalisation. No first EU AI strict liability case filed as of reporting window.

algorithmic_accountability.item: FTC enforcement — Air AI settlement and endorsement guidance
algorithmic_accountability.summary: Beyond Air AI settlement, FTC updated AI-generated endorsement disclosure guidance on 12 March. FTC remains the most active federal AI enforcement body in the absence of comprehensive federal AI legislation.

algorithmic_accountability.item: Texas HB 1709 (AI risk assessments) advanced through the House
algorithmic_accountability.summary: Requires risk assessments for high-risk AI use in HR, credit, and insurance. Georgia SB 392 (government AI transparency) passed both chambers, requiring state agencies to disclose AI systems used and for what purposes.

governance_gaps.analysis: The White House framework opposes creating a new federal AI regulator; the EU AI Act's implementation delays create a multi-year window of unclear and unenforced obligations. Labs operating in both jurisdictions have a structural incentive to use the US governance vacuum as a 'home base' for the most controversial capabilities, serving EU markets through compliant deployment wrappers. This 'regulatory arbitrage by deployer interposition' is not currently prohibited by either framework.
governance_gaps.asymmetric: Anthropic's RSP v3.0 removal of its unilateral pause commitment is a leading indicator: a voluntary governance commitment that is weakened when competitive pressure rises is not a governance commitment — it is a signalling device. Binding international AI governance agreements are the only plausible route to meaningful safety floors — and the US/EU governance divergence makes those agreements very difficult to achieve.

items.title: Anthropic RSP v3.0 — accountability friction
items.accountability_friction: Anthropic's RSP v3.0 removes the unilateral commitment not to deploy models posing unacceptable risks, replacing it with an 'industry-wide recommendation.' GovAI notes other companies modelled safety frameworks on Anthropic's RSP — the downgrade creates cascading permission to soften voluntary commitments across the industry. Increased transparency mechanisms (Risk Reports, Frontier Safety Roadmap) introduced simultaneously, but the consequential enforcement lever (pause commitment) removed. The friction: between Anthropic's 'safety-first lab' positioning and the operational reality that competitive pressure caused it to lower the functional deployment threshold it previously committed to treating as a categorical halt.
items.asymmetric: Anthropic's pause commitment was the most credible unilateral safety backstop in the frontier AI industry. Its removal signals no lab will accept unilateral competitive disadvantage in exchange for safety guarantees that competitors will not match. This is the market structure failure that makes binding international AI governance the only plausible route to meaningful safety floors.

items.title: OpenAI Pentagon contract — ethics-by-contract limits
items.accountability_friction: OpenAI's agreement with the Pentagon allows 'any lawful use' subject to references to Fourth Amendment, FISA, and National Security Act. Lawfare's analysis establishes that these references shift interpretation entirely to the Pentagon: the government is the first and controlling interpreter of what the law permits in the moment of use. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman told employees: 'You do not not make operational decisions.' This directly contradicts OpenAI's stated safety principles while contractually transferring operational control to the DoD.
items.asymmetric: The OpenAI 'any lawful use' contract model is now the industry template for military AI procurement. Every subsequent frontier lab that signs a DoD contract under this framework has functionally delegated their safety commitments to Pentagon legal interpretation. By 2028, multiple frontier AI systems will operate in classified military environments with no contractual recourse for the developing labs.

items.title: FTC v. Air AI — enforcement precedent
items.accountability_friction: FTC's first completed enforcement action targeting deceptive AI business opportunity marketing. $18M judgment (primarily suspended). Structural significance: FTC's demonstrated willingness to use existing Section 5 and Business Opportunity Rule authority against AI-marketing claims without waiting for new AI-specific legislation.
items.asymmetric: FTC's pattern of enforcement using existing authority in the absence of AI-specific legislation will accelerate through 2026 midterms and into 2027, establishing precedent that will inform any eventual comprehensive federal AI consumer protection framework.

items.title: Anthropic Social Policy Institute launch
items.accountability_friction: ASPI launched 13 March, led by co-founder Jack Clark. An institute funded by the AI company it studies cannot produce fully independent accountability assessments. The structural incentive is for ASPI to produce research that validates Anthropic's deployment decisions. The pattern — Google's PAIR, Microsoft's AETHER, now Anthropic's ASPI — suggests the industry is converging on in-house ethics infrastructure as the preferred alternative to external regulation.
items.asymmetric: Lab-funded 'ethics institutes' are reputational management mechanisms. They generate publishable research, provide regulatory credibility, and create the appearance of independent scrutiny without the structural independence that generates genuine accountability.

module_12.title: Information Operations

items.title: EEAS 4th Annual FIMI Threats Report — AI normalisation
items.summary: 540 detected global FIMI incidents in 2025; 10,500 social media channels and websites mobilised; 1 in 4 incidents involved AI tools. Russia (29%) and China (6%) attributed for 35%. China's Spamouflage deployed AI deepfake videos against dissidents; Paperwall expanded by 108 new domains across 40 countries in 15 new languages. HR/VP Kaja Kallas: 'marketplace for manipulation' where influence operations are outsourced to private contractors financed through cryptocurrency.
items.asymmetric: The 25% AI-incident rate from 2025 will not be a ceiling. Current attribution frameworks based on detectable infrastructure markers will degrade as AI enables rapid infrastructure rotation and persona generation.

items.title: AI deepfakes in 2026 US midterm election campaigns
items.summary: Reuters (28 March): National Republican Senatorial Committee ran AI-generated video depicting Democratic candidate making statements he never made, with disclosure in barely visible small text. No federal law restricts AI in political advertising. Meta and X flag some AI-generated political content but have abandoned professional fact-checking.
items.asymmetric: The 2026 midterms are the first US federal election cycle with commercially accessible, photorealistic deepfake capability at scale. The absence of federal disclosure requirements means the only constraint is platform policy — currently inconsistent and weakening. Damage to public trust in political media will be structural and persistent, not reversible by legislation.

items.title: Russia — coordinated disinformation targeting Armenian elections
items.summary: Fake video circulated on X depicting events around Armenia's political landscape with markers consistent with Russian FIMI tactics. Attack patterns from Moldova's 2024 elections emerging in Armenia ahead of June 2026 parliamentary elections. EEAS FIMI report explicitly flags Armenia as expected future target.
items.asymmetric: The EEAS's explicit forward-assessment identifying Armenia as a high-probability FIMI target represents a rare instance of predictive public attribution. This kind of early warning, if acted upon, could establish a model for pre-emptive FIMI countermeasures.

items.title: Meta H1 2026 Adversarial Threat Report — FIMI/fraud convergence
items.summary: Meta's H1 2026 ATR documents convergence between influence operations and financial fraud/scam networks — operators running FIMI campaigns using the same AI-generated infrastructure for financial exploitation. NewsGuard identified coordinated AI-generated TikTok content deployed to manipulate Hungary's 2026 elections.
items.asymmetric: Platforms optimised for fraud detection (banks, payment processors) are now sitting on the most commercially valuable FIMI detection datasets. By 2027, financial intelligence community-led FIMI detection may outpace journalism and civil society detection — raising questions about who controls that intelligence and how it flows to democratic oversight.

capability_watch.detail: EU DisinfoLab webinar (26 March): DSA's host/creator distinction that generative AI dissolves. Generative AI providers producing synthetic political content cannot be treated as 'hosts' under DSA's safe harbour — facing both potential liability as content creators and regulatory uncertainty about which framework applies. Berlin court ordered X to provide civil society researchers DSA data access — first national court enforcement of DSA data access rights.

asymmetric_flags: 25% AI-incident rate in FIMI will not be a ceiling — it is a floor.
The 2026 midterms are the live-fire stress test for post-truth electoral environments; findings will reshape legislative priorities in 2027.

Russia's predictive targeting of Armenia ahead of June 2026 elections provides a model for pre-emptive countermeasures — if institutional capacity and platform cooperation exist to act on the warning.
FIMI/financial fraud convergence means fraud detection infrastructure is now the most commercially valuable FIMI detection layer.

module_13.title: AI & Society

items.title: Anthropic research: 14% drop in job entry rates for young knowledge workers
items.affected_groups: Young knowledge workers (22–25), computer programmers, customer service, financial analysts
items.summary: Anthropic research (5 March) using 'observed exposure' measure combining LLM capability with real-world automated usage data. Key finding: tentative evidence of a 14% drop in job entry rates for workers aged 22–25 in most-exposed occupations post-ChatGPT. No statistically significant impact on unemployment rates — suggesting displaced young workers may exit the labour force rather than appear as unemployed. Goldman Sachs: 300 million global jobs exposed to AI automation; 6–7% worker displacement over 10-year transition if adoption is gradual.
items.asymmetric: The 14% reduction in job entry rates for young workers in exposed occupations is the most consequential short-term labour market signal in this research. A cohort of workers entering the knowledge economy between 2023–2028 will have permanently reduced lifetime earnings with no compensation mechanism — creating a structurally aggrieved workforce cohort that will be politically mobilised around AI by 2030.

items.title: AI literacy legislation wave — 52 bills across 25 US states
items.summary: FutureEd tracking 53 bills across 25 US states in 2026 session. Three enacted: Idaho SB 1227, Utah HB 218, Utah HB 273. South Carolina HB 5253 would establish strongest statewide guardrails (written parental opt-in, annual public AI disclosure, prohibition on AI replacing licensed teachers). AI literacy to be assessed on 2029 PISA for the first time — creating international benchmarking mechanism.
items.asymmetric: 2029 PISA AI literacy inclusion will politically embarrass low-scoring countries and drive rapid curriculum adoption regardless of pedagogical evidence. Countries building AI literacy into core curricula now compound their AI talent pipeline advantage into the 2030s.

items.title: Hoover Institution panels: 'velocity mismatch' between AI pace and political systems
items.summary: Hoover panels (17 March) with scholars, industry, and former senior policymakers. Panellists cited reskilling systems that are 'too slow, too fragmented, and too disconnected from employer practices.' Fei-Fei Li, when asked about the horizon for AI change: 'You mean in six weeks?' — underscoring the pace mismatch between political/educational cycles and AI advancement tempo.
items.asymmetric: The velocity mismatch Li identified is the structural precondition for a public trust crisis. When visible AI-induced harms accumulate faster than policy responses, political backlash will not be proportionate or technically calibrated — it will be blunt. Labs that have not built genuine accountability infrastructure by the time the backlash arrives will face the most severe regulatory overreach.

items.title: WEF: AI-enabled disinformation as destabilising systemic force
items.affected_groups: Voters, democratic institutions, minority communities targeted by FIMI
items.summary: WEF analysis (12 March): 'In 2026, the widespread information disorder is a destabilising systemic force that has the potential to disrupt democracies, erode social cohesion and make existing problems worse.' WEF Global Risks Report 2026 placed mis/disinformation among top short-term global risks — one of few risks remaining severe over both two- and ten-year horizons.
items.asymmetric: The WEF notes that simply knowing deepfakes exist causes people to doubt genuine evidence — the 'liar's dividend.' This epistemological damage is cumulative and non-reversible by better detection technology alone. By 2028, populations in high-FIMI-exposure countries will have experienced multi-year exposure to AI-synthetic media at scale; long-term effects on political trust and institutional legitimacy are unknown and unlikely to be benign.

structural_trends: Young worker labour market displacement is occurring through reduced job entry, not increased unemployment — making it invisible to standard economic indicators.
AI literacy legislative wave is state-level and fragmented; federal White House framework favours embedding AI into incumbent platforms over creating new standards.
Velocity mismatch between AI capability development and political/institutional adaptation is the root cause of impending public trust crisis — not any specific AI capability.
asymmetric_flags: 14% reduction in job entry for young workers (22–25) in exposed occupations is a generational cohort effect — structurally aggrieved workforce mobilised around AI by 2030.
2029 PISA AI literacy inclusion will drive curriculum adoption faster than any voluntary programme — countries behind now will compound educational deficit into 2030s.
Liar's dividend from deepfake proliferation is epistemologically non-reversible; cumulative trust damage exceeds any individual incident.

module_14.title: AI & Power Structures

items.title: Hyperscaler infrastructure lock-in — $750B data center capex in 2026
items.summary: BloombergNEF (24 March): capex from 14 largest publicly owned data center operators tracking toward $750B in 2026 — two-thirds increase from FY2025 (itself a two-thirds increase from FY2024). Hyperscalers (Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Meta) accounted for 72% of clean power PPAs in the Americas in 2025, effectively cornering the supply of renewable energy capacity in key markets. Morgan Stanley estimates top 4–5 hyperscalers will spend $630B on data centers and AI chips in 2026 alone.
items.asymmetric: Hyperscaler energy infrastructure lock-in — through dedicated nuclear restart agreements and long-term PPAs — is creating a two-tier power grid: one serving frontier AI data centers at scale and reliability, one serving everyone else. By 2028, this energy infrastructure moat will be the primary barrier to entry for new frontier AI competitors, more durable than chip access or model architecture advantages.

items.title: SpaceX–xAI merger — vertical integration of AI + space + government contracts
items.summary: SpaceX acquired xAI (February 2026), combining Grok LLM, Starlink global satellite internet, SpaceX defense/NASA contracts, and X (Twitter) social media platform — all privately held by an individual serving as executive branch senior advisor. Combined entity valued over $1 trillion. No single regulator has comprehensive oversight: EU AI Act, FTC, FCC, NASA/DoD procurement rules all have jurisdictional interests in components.
items.asymmetric: The SpaceX–xAI stack creates a model for 'sovereign AI infrastructure' — a privately-held entity controlling all layers from satellite bandwidth to model inference — that will be studied and emulated by national governments. The precedent it sets for permissible concentration in AI-adjacent sectors will shape regulatory norms globally.

items.title: EU competition chief activates AI energy stack scrutiny
items.summary: Teresa Ribera (12 March): regulators examining the entire AI stack — applications, models, training data, cloud infrastructure, and energy sources — for competition distortions. Named energy infrastructure as a contested enforcement layer, moving EU antitrust scrutiny from model/application layers into the compute-energy stack.
items.asymmetric: EU competition chief's activation of energy-layer scrutiny will propagate distributed enforcement across jurisdictions. Federal settlement of US antitrust cases does not close EU proceedings. A finding of competition distortion in energy infrastructure procurement would reach every hyperscaler operating in EU territory.

items.title: Pentagon procurement as regulatory capture mechanism
items.summary: Anthropic blacklisting and OpenAI contract precedents establish a structural regulatory capture dynamic: DoD using procurement power to select AI labs based on willingness to accept government interpretation of permissible use. Labs accepting 'any lawful use' contracts gain access to defense market; labs insisting on categorical prohibitions are designated national security risks. Contracting functions as a regulatory mechanism outside Congressional AI legislation or normal judicial oversight.
items.asymmetric: The 'governance by contract' model will migrate from defense to civilian government procurement. By 2027, federal agencies adopting AI systems under 'any lawful use' contracts will have delegated ethical boundary-setting for AI in public services to contractors' interpretation of applicable law — not democratic oversight processes.

concentration_index.note: Nvidia ~11x AMD in AI data center revenue. Frore Systems (thermal management) and Ecolab (cooling infrastructure) emerging as chokepoint layers below the chip.
5–6 frontier labs controlling SOTA. SpaceX–xAI merger consolidates further. AMI Labs (LeCun) represents first credible architectural challenger to transformer paradigm.
Big 4 capex approaching $750B in 2026. 72% of clean power PPAs in Americas from hyperscalers. Energy infrastructure lock-in becoming the durable competitive moat.
Application layer remains competitive; commoditisation accelerating as inference costs drop. Harvey AI and Legora demonstrating platform lock-in dynamics in legal vertical.
Safety infrastructure concentrating among the same actors being overseen — structural independence deficit. Anthropic RSP v3.0 weakening voluntary commitments further reduces effective oversight.

asymmetric_flags: Energy infrastructure moat (nuclear PPAs, dedicated generation) is the 2028 primary barrier to entry — more durable than chip access or model architecture advantages.
SpaceX–xAI vertical integration has no regulatory precedent; the permissible concentration norm it establishes will shape global regulatory responses.
Pentagon 'governance by contract' model migrating from defense to civilian government procurement — democratic accountability for AI in public services at risk.
EU competition chief's activation of energy-layer AI stack scrutiny is a multi-year enforcement signal that reaches every hyperscaler operating in EU territory.

module_15.title: Personnel & Org Watch

lab_movements.name: Caitlin Kalinowski
lab_movements.significance: Most senior OpenAI exit explicitly tied to military AI governance concerns since founding. Resigned over Pentagon deal governance process: 'These are too important for deals or announcements to be rushed.' Over 30 OpenAI and Google DeepMind employees signed statements supporting Anthropic's DoD lawsuit the same week.
lab_movements.asymmetric: Ethics-motivated departures from AI labs over military contracts create a talent market signal: researchers and engineers with genuine safety convictions are being displaced from frontier labs into academia, civil society, or smaller organisations. This hollows out internal dissent capacity at the labs most likely to require it — precisely as military AI deployments accelerate.

lab_movements.name: Max Schwarzer
lab_movements.significance: Anthropic remains the primary destination for departing OpenAI research leadership — maintaining research capability pipeline relative to OpenAI.
lab_movements.asymmetric: Anthropic's ability to recruit senior research talent from OpenAI despite offering lower compensation in some cases signals that research independence and mission alignment remain powerful non-monetary recruiting levers. If Anthropic's Pentagon litigation succeeds, this pipeline could accelerate further.

lab_movements.name: Scott Rosecrans
lab_movements.role_from: VP AI Sales and Strategic Pursuits, AWS
lab_movements.significance: OpenAI is building enterprise sales infrastructure specifically designed for large-scale government and defense procurement. Hiring from AWS's AI sales leadership targets the exact customer segment OpenAI is expanding into.
lab_movements.asymmetric: AWS→OpenAI talent flow in sales (rather than research) signals OpenAI's 2026 strategic priority: commercial and government revenue scale over pure research advancement — consistent with the 'any lawful use' contract posture and representing a corporate culture shift that is difficult to reverse once embedded.

lab_movements.name: David Luan
lab_movements.role_from: VP, Head of AGI Lab and Agentic AI R&D, AWS
lab_movements.significance: Departed Amazon after less than two years following Amazon's acquisition of his team from Adept AI Labs — signals potential turbulence in Amazon's AGI lab strategy.
lab_movements.asymmetric: AWS's successive senior AI departures (Luan, Rosecrans) while simultaneously deepening OpenAI partnership suggests Amazon may be shifting from internal capability development to commercial partnership with frontier labs — concentrating model development further at OpenAI/Anthropic.

lab_movements.name: Jack Clark
lab_movements.role_to: Head of Public Benefit; Director, Anthropic Social Policy Institute
lab_movements.significance: Clark is one of the longest-serving and most credible voices in AI safety advocacy (co-founder of Import AI, former OpenAI policy lead). His formal positioning at ASPI gives Anthropic's public benefit claims significant reputational credibility.
lab_movements.asymmetric: Clark's ASPI appointment and the Anthropic–Pentagon litigation are complementary messaging strategies: ASPI signals commitment to public benefit; litigation signals willingness to resist government coercion. Together they position Anthropic as the 'responsible frontier lab' in contrast to OpenAI's 'any lawful use' posture. Whether this positioning survives the competitive pressures that caused RSP v3.0's pause commitment to be dropped is the central question for Anthropic's governance trajectory.

government_ai_bodies.body: AISI Pipeline — No confirmed senior movements this week
government_ai_bodies.detail: No Tier 1/2 confirmed reports of senior AISI (UK AI Security Institute), US NIST AI Safety, Canadian CAISI, or EU AI Office departures to frontier labs during 23–29 March window. UK AI Safety Institute was rebranded as AI Security Institute in February 2025. International Network of AI Safety Institutes rebranded as International Network for Advanced AI Measurement Evaluation and Science in December 2025, with UK as coordinator.
government_ai_bodies.note: Structural changes make senior personnel tracking more complex as role descriptions and institutional hierarchies are still settling.

revolving_door.observation: Ethics-motivated departures creating 'safety brain drain' from frontier labs
revolving_door.detail: Kalinowski (OpenAI hardware/robotics), plus 30+ employee signatories supporting Anthropic's DoD lawsuit, signal systematic displacement of safety-oriented talent from the labs where it is most needed. Pattern accelerates as military AI deployments increase in scope and speed.
revolving_door.12_month_implication: Internal dissent capacity at frontier labs hollowed out precisely as capabilities become most consequential.

revolving_door.observation: Alibaba appoints ex-DeepMind/Gemini researcher to lead Qwen post-training
revolving_door.detail: Chinese labs systematically recruiting researchers with direct frontier model experience from Google and OpenAI — not merely Chinese-origin researchers returning from US.
revolving_door.12_month_implication: Chinese lab capability acceleration in H2 2026 likely as direct frontier model institutional knowledge is applied to Qwen4 development.

asymmetric_flags: AISI pipeline tracking now complicated by institutional rebranding — the monitoring gap itself is a governance signal.
AWS→OpenAI sales talent flow signals OpenAI's 2026 strategic pivot from research to government revenue at scale.
Ethics-motivated lab departures will continue as military AI deployments accelerate through 2026 — watch for these as leading indicators of internal lab governance deterioration.
Alibaba CEO direct assumption of AI group + DeepMind post-training hire = board-level acceleration signal for Qwen4.

delta_strip.title: Ciyuan: China formalises AI tokens as settlement currency
delta_strip.one_line: China's National Data Administration officially designates the AI 'token' as an economic settlement unit, framing it as a value anchor for the intelligent era — the first move in a potential token-denominated AI trade framework targeting Global South markets.

delta_strip.title: OpenAI kills Sora/Disney — redirects compute to robotics pre-training
delta_strip.one_line: Not a content pivot. A signal that video-synthesis compute is being reallocated to embodied AI pre-training — labs that read this correctly now have a 12-month robotics capability lead.

delta_strip.title: Langflow CVE-2026-33017 exploited within 20 hours — AI orchestration stack now established attack surface
delta_strip.one_line: CISA KEV addition confirms agentic AI orchestration infrastructure (Langflow, LiteLLM, n8n) is the new enterprise attack surface — a single compromised orchestration layer gives lateral access across the entire connected data estate.

delta_strip.title: Ecolab $4.75B CoolIT acquisition — water company becomes AI infrastructure gatekeeper
delta_strip.one_line: A $74B water treatment company paying 17x the prior investment price for a 5% market penetration cooling business is the clearest signal yet that physical thermal management is the next AI infrastructure moat.

delta_strip.title: The AI Scientist — first AI-authored paper passes peer review; Nature calls for governance response
delta_strip.one_line: Quality scales with model capability — this system becomes dramatically more capable as frontier models improve. Nature's call for institutional response signals the scientific establishment recognises a phase transition that governance is weeks to months behind.

country_grid.signal: OpenAI Sora cancelled; Pentagon Anthropic injunction; White House nonbinding AI framework released; Shield AI $2B; CISA KEV AI infrastructure vulnerabilities.
Parliament 569–45–23 Omnibus vote; trilogue open; Standards Vacuum confirmed; Ribera AI stack competition scrutiny activated.
Copyright reform deferred; DSIT £40M Fundamental AI Research Lab; AISI no senior pipeline movements.
Ciyuan formalised as settlement unit; DeepSeek V4 still pending; Zhipu GLM-5 Ascend-native frontier capability confirmed; Alibaba CEO takes direct AI group control.
AMI Labs $1.03B seed (LeCun/JEPA); Bpifrance government alignment signal.
Legora $550M Series D; US expansion begins.
BSI alert on LiteLLM/Trivy supply chain attack; Solaris 20% staff cut to become 'AI-native bank'.

country_grid_watch.signal: EEAS explicitly flags Armenia as high-probability FIMI target ahead of June 2026 elections; Russia-attributed deepfake video already circulating on X.
NewsGuard documented coordinated AI-generated TikTok content promoting pro-Orbán messaging ahead of elections.

flags.title: AI-enabled FIMI: shared operational layer between AI Governance and FIMI monitors
flags.linkage: AI Governance (M12) and the FIMI & Cognitive Warfare monitor share a core operational subject: the use of generative AI tools in foreign information manipulation. The EEAS 4th Annual FIMI Report confirms 25% of 2025 incidents involved AI tools. AI deepfakes are now confirmed in 2026 US midterm campaigns. The FIMI monitor tracks campaigns and actor attribution; the AI Governance monitor tracks the underlying capability layer enabling them.
flags.ai_governance_perspective: The capability side — generative AI cost curves, platform responses, detection tool accuracy — is our domain. The FIMI monitor provides the attribution and campaign-level view that contextualises whether AI capability improvements are being absorbed into operational FIMI infrastructure.

version_history.reason: EEAS FIMI report + US midterm deepfakes confirmed

flags.title: AI deepfakes in 2026 US midterms: AI capability meeting democratic integrity stress
flags.linkage: Reuters confirmed (28 March) that AI-generated deepfake advertisements are running in 2026 US midterm campaigns with no federal disclosure requirement. The Democratic Integrity monitor tracks democratic erosion indicators; this monitor identified the specific capability vector — commercially accessible photorealistic deepfakes at scale — being deployed into the electoral environment it monitors.
flags.ai_governance_perspective: The absence of federal disclosure requirements is a governance gap we track. The Democratic Integrity monitor provides the downstream democratic-erosion framing that our capability analysis implies but does not fully develop.

version_history.reason: Reuters confirmed AI deepfakes in midterm campaigns

flags.title: Military AI procurement and autonomous weapons: AI capability meeting conflict escalation
flags.linkage: AI Governance (M08) tracks the Anduril/Palantir Golden Dome C2 contract, the Anthropic Pentagon injunction, and the DoD 'any lawful use' contract model. Palantir has described the Iran War (commenced Feb 2026) as 'the first major AI-driven conflict.' The two monitors share the military AI deployment layer — AI Governance from the procurement and doctrine angle, Strategic Conflict from the operational and escalation angle.
flags.ai_governance_perspective: Our IHL friction analysis — DoD Directive 3000.09 'meaningful human control' vs hypersonic engagement timelines — maps directly to escalation dynamics the conflict monitor tracks. If autonomous engagement systems are deployed in active theatres, our governance gap analysis becomes operationally predictive.

version_history.reason: Golden Dome + Anthropic Pentagon injunction + Iran War AI-driven characterisation

monitors_involved: Global Environmental Risks & Planetary Boundaries Monitor
flags.title: AI energy infrastructure and grid constraint: compute sovereignty meets planetary boundaries
flags.linkage: AI Governance (M03 Energy Wall, M14 Concentration Index) tracks $750B hyperscaler capex, 49 GW US data center power shortfall by 2028, and hyperscaler nuclear PPA lock-in. Hyperscalers now account for 72% of clean power PPAs in the Americas, cornering renewable energy supply. The Environmental Risks monitor covers planetary boundary status and geopolitical cascades from environmental stress — the energy infrastructure decisions being locked in now for AI compute will materially affect grid stability, energy transition timelines, and carbon accounting.
flags.ai_governance_perspective: We track who controls compute infrastructure and how energy lock-in creates competitive moats. The Environmental Risks monitor provides the planetary-boundary context for whether AI's energy demand trajectory is compatible with climate commitments.

version_history.reason: EIA grid constraint data + hyperscaler PPA dominance confirmed

flags.title: EU AI Act and European AI sovereignty: regulatory framework as strategic instrument
flags.linkage: AI Governance (M05, M09) tracks the EU AI Act Omnibus trilogue (EP voted 569–45–23, 26 March), the Standards Vacuum (2 August 2026 cliff), and AMI Labs' €890M seed (LeCun/JEPA) as Europe's most significant sovereign AI bet. European Strategic Autonomy covers the geopolitical contest over European independence — the AI regulatory framework and the emergence of European frontier AI labs are core strategic autonomy questions. The AI Act is simultaneously a sovereignty instrument and a compliance burden.
flags.ai_governance_perspective: The Standards Vacuum we track is not just a compliance problem — it is a strategic vulnerability. European companies facing compliance obligations without technical standards are disadvantaged relative to US labs that have no equivalent binding framework. Whether Europe's regulatory investment translates into strategic AI advantage is the question both monitors share.

version_history.reason: Omnibus vote + AMI Labs + Standards Vacuum active

flags.title: AI investment concentration and systemic financial risk
flags.linkage: AI Governance (M03, M14) tracks OpenAI's rolling $120B+ round at $730–840B valuation with $14B projected 2026 loss, Anthropic's $380B valuation, and hyperscaler capex approaching $750B in 2026. The Macro Monitor tracks debt, credit, market structure, and systemic risk. The AI investment concentration — SoftBank, sovereign wealth funds, and major institutional allocators all exposed — is now large enough to have systemic implications if confidence breaks. The rolling-commitment structure has converted frontier AI funding from venture capital into quasi-sovereign infrastructure financing.
flags.ai_governance_perspective: We track investment signals for what they reveal about strategic priorities and competitive dynamics. The Macro Monitor provides the financial stability framing: whether these valuation structures are sustainable and what a correction would mean for the AI infrastructure buildout.

version_history.reason: OpenAI $120B+ rolling round; $14B projected loss; hyperscaler capex $750B

version_history.change: Initialised with 6 active flags covering all 6 live monitors on asym-intel.info
